mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rapid labor market deterioration signals recessionary momentum, compressing earnings expectations and driving negative SPX returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >60% of triggered instances despite the UNRATE threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in commercial credit (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates a synchronized demand shock, depressing corporate cash flows and yielding negative SPX returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: Joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST does not predict negative 60d forward returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects supply chain normalization and capacity utilization gains, driving positive SPX returns over the next 5 trading days during post-shock recoveries.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to produce positive 5d forward returns in >70% of observations during post-shock periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp GDP contraction triggers earnings downgrade cycles, causing negative SPX forward returns over 20 trading days as market pricing adjusts to lower corporate profits.
failure_condition: GDP contractions exceeding 2% annualized do not lead to negative 20d forward returns in the majority of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Coordinated decline in FEDFUNDS alongside stable CPIAUCSL signals effective monetary easing without inflationary pressure, compressing real discount rates and generating positive SPX returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: Joint FEDFUNDS decline and CPIAUCSL stability do not predict positive 60d forward returns (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising employment levels indicate improving consumer spending conditions, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive SPX returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS increases do not correlate with positive 5d forward returns (Spearman correlation < 0.02 over 3 years).

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts reflect bottoming consumer demand and wealth effects, producing positive SPX returns over 20 trading days as equity markets price in economic recovery.
failure_condition: HOUST increases during housing stabilization periods fail to yield positive 20d forward returns in >55% of instances.
